calendar.matters:
Petition For 1) Order Authorizing Payment Of Attorneys Fees And For Reimbursement To Attorneys For Costs Advanced, 2) Order Allowing Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account, And 3) An Amended Order Granting Petition To Determine Heirship And Approving Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared by zoom for Petitioners.

The Court stated that the only controversial issue is the request to amend the order to allow automatic distribution when the Minor turns 18. Mr. Vaught stated that two of the Minors turn 18 next month, that they both intend to use the money to attend college and that the prior orders have no release dates. The Court explained that an ex parte petition is usually required to release the funds, that it is not inclined to amend the order and that Mr. Vaught can file an ex parte petition. Mr. Vaught raised the issue of additional fees for the ex parte, and stated that he is trying to avoid another filing fee and a fee to him. The Petition for Order Authorizing Payment of Attorney Fees for Guardian Ad Litem, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For 1) Order Authorizing Payment Of Attorneys Fees And For Reimbursement To Attorneys For Costs Advanced, 2) Order Allowing Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account, And 3) An Amended Order Granting Petition To Determine Heirship And Approving Settlement Agreement

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Final Distribution To Vienna Puccinelli
Off Calendar Mar-26-2020 Continued To Aug-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-20-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ellen I. Kahn for Sandra J. Puccinelli.

The Court stated before it today is the Second Amended First And Final Report Of Administrator On Waiver Of Account; Allowance Of Compensation; Repayment Of Obligation; To Retain Minor's Share Until Age 18; Reduce Bond; And For Final Distribution Other Than Retained Minor's Share. The Court inquired whether Ms. Kahn had received the probate examiner's notes. Ms. Kahn confirmed that she had received the notes. The Court stated that it was inclined to grant the petition and "allow" not approve the distribution. Ms. Kahn stated that they would send in the receipts for the distributions and would then file an ex parte petition to reduce the bond. The Court stated that the petition for final distribution would be filed after Vienna Puccinelli reaches the age of majority. The Court then granted the Second Amended First And Final Report Of Administrator On Waiver Of Account; Second Amended Petition For Allowance Of Compensation To Attorneys For Ordinary Services And For Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced; Second Amended Petition For Repayment Of Obligation To Sandra J. Puccinelli By Her Children; Second Amended Petition For Authority To Retain Minor's Share Until Age 18; Second Amended Petition To Reduce Bond; and Second Amended Petition For Final Distribution Other Than Retained Minor's Share (subject to review of the order) with modifications. The status on the filing of the final distribution to Vienna Puccinelli is set for June 1, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-08-2019 Continued To Aug-12-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ellen Kahn for Sandra Puccinelli.

Ms. Kahn provided a copy of a receipt for payment of the $450.00 filing fee, stated that when they went back in they realized there was an undiscovered asset, that they will need to file a further inventory and that they will have to file another amended petition. Ms. Kahn also stated that the Minor becomes of age in April of 2020, that all of the assets are interests in LLC's, that they originally requested to distribute to the Guardian Ad Litem, that they were told by the Court that they can not do that and that the Probate Examiner suggested keeping the estate open. In addition, Ms. Kahn stated that the asset was an interest in the estate of the Decedent's Father, that the Referee indicated he/she could have the inventory done by the middle of next week and that she will have waivers of notice. The Amended First and Final Report of Administrator on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to August 12, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Off Calendar
Ex Parte Application For Order Amending Granting Petition To Determine Heirship And Approving Settlement Agreement

Petition To Determine Heirship And Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonna Thomas for petitioner Genevieve Puccinelli; Ellen Kahn for administrator Sandra Puccinelli; and Jon Vaught for Guardian ad litem Charles Gelman.

The Court inquird into the reason Mr. Gelman had signed the settlement agreement on behalf of the minors prior to the date of his appointment. Mr. Vaught responded that his office had submitted ex parte applications for appointment as guardian ad litem eearly in the year, and prior to the mediation which produced the settlement agreement had seen an entry in the register of actions of an order for appointment of guardian ad litem which had erroneously been supposed to be for the appointment of Mr. Gelman. After the mediation it was discovered that the order did not apply to Mr. Gelman, so a new ex parte application for appointment of Mr. GAL was then submitted and granted.
The Court expressed concern over the requested finding that the Puccinelli children are the sole heirs as children of the decedent Robert Puccinelli, without any evidence of that before the court. Ms. Thomas replied that subsequest to the death of Mr. Puccinelli it was learned that he apparently had another family in secret. The two families had worked through this challenging situation to come to an agreement in settlement of the respective claims, and that it wewas a condition of the settlement agreement that the Court make the determination that the Puccinelli children are the decedent's sole heirs as children. She stated that no one else has ever claimed any heirship rights, and the decedent never held himself out as a parent of any other child during his lifetime. She added that the petition for heirship was unopposed, and the Court has authority to make the requested finding under Probate Code section 11700. Mr. Vaught stated that there was evidence to support the requested finding, as Probate Code section 102.2 provides that a verified petition is evidence in an unopposed proceeding. Ms. Thomas added that her client would object to any order which modifies the relief sought by the petition.
Mr Vaught requested that the Court allow CUTMA accounts in excess of $100,000 so that $137,000 could be placed in each of the two accounts, to enable the expenses for the care of the minors to be available as needed. Alternatively, he requested that $100,000 be placed in a CUTMA account and $37,000 in a blocked account for each child. Ms. Thomas also opposed placing the entirety of the $137,000 per child being placed in blocked accounts. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not reported)

Petition To Determine Heirship And Approve Settlement Agreement

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught for Charles Gellman (Petitioner).
Ellen Kahn for Sandra Puccinelli (Petitioner).

Mr. Vaught stated that they withdraw their petition in light of the fact that the Surviving Spouse filed her petition. The Court granted Ms. Puccinelli's Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $5,504,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for May 15, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Vaught stated that his Client intends to file an ex parte petition for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor Children.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
